pullquote_provenance.speaker: Teamed Legal Operations
pullquote_provenance.note: Anonymised to 'Teamed Legal Operations' per memory rule (feedback_pullquote_provenance). No MI extraction found for Michigan ESTA/PFML topic. Quote reflects in-house view on ESTA history (HB 4002), the two-tier threshold error pattern, and the PFML absence. No individual named, no MI extraction_id required.

factual_uncertainty_flags.note: ESTA and HB 4002 have been subject to ongoing legal challenges since the 2018 initiative. As of 2026-05-30, the law is in effect as described. Monitor for any appellate court decisions affecting the two-tier structure or the small-employer carryover cap. Michigan LEO ESTA FAQ is the primary real-time source.

Michigan LEO's formal rulemaking on documentation requirements (specifically what constitutes 'reasonable documentation' for absences over 3 consecutive days) was still developing as of early 2026. Page reflects statutory text and agency FAQ guidance; re-verify against final LEO rules when published.

The 'three years from first hire, or until 11+ employees' new-business transition rule under HB 4002 has limited agency guidance on edge cases (e.g. employer that crosses 11 employees in year 2). Page reflects statutory text. Verify against LEO FAQ updates for ambiguous scenarios.
